---
slug: integrations/third-party/pydantic-ai
subtitle: Use Confident AI for LLM observability and evals for PydanticAI
---

## Overview

[Pydantic AI](https://ai.pydantic.dev/) is a Python-native LLM agent framework built on the foundations of Pydantic validation. Confident AI allows you to trace and evaluate Pydantic AI agents using an OpenTelemetry-based integration.

## Tracing Quickstart

<Tip>
For users in the EU region, please set the OTEL endpoint to the EU version:

```bash
export CONFIDENT_OTEL_URL="https://eu.otel.confident-ai.com"
```

</Tip>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Install Dependencies">
    Run the following command to install the required packages:
    ```bash
    pip install -U deepeval pydantic-ai
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Setup Confident AI Key">
    Login to Confident AI using your Confident API key.

    <CodeBlocks>

    <CodeBlock title="Env" language="bash">

    ```bash
    export CONFIDENT_API_KEY="<your-confident-api-key>"
    ```

    </CodeBlock>

    <CodeBlock language="bash" title="Bash">

    ```bash
    deepeval login
    ```

    </CodeBlock>

    <CodeBlock language="python">

    ```python
    import deepeval

    deepeval.login("<your-confident-api-key>")
    ```

    </CodeBlock>

    </CodeBlocks>

  </Step>

  <Step title="Configure Pydantic AI">
    Pass `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings` to your agent's `instrument` parameter. This sets up the full OpenTelemetry pipeline — including span classification, trace context wiring, and export to Confident AI — in a single step.

    <Tabs>
      <Tab title="Synchronous">
        ```python main.py
        from pydantic_ai import Agent
        from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

        agent = Agent(
            "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
            system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
            name="my_agent",
            instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
        )

        result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
        print(result.output)
        ```
      </Tab>
      <Tab title="Asynchronous">
        ```python main.py
        import asyncio
        from pydantic_ai import Agent
        from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

        agent = Agent(
            "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
            system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
            name="my_agent",
            instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
        )

        async def main():
            result = await agent.run("What are LLMs?")
            print(result.output)

        asyncio.run(main())
        ```
      </Tab>
      <Tab title="Streaming">
        ```python main.py
        import asyncio
        from pydantic_ai import Agent
        from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

        agent = Agent(
            "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
            system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
            name="my_agent",
            instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
        )

        async def main():
            async with agent.run_stream("What is the weather in London?") as result:
                async for chunk in result.stream_text(delta=True):
                    print(chunk, end="", flush=True)
                final = await result.get_output()
                print("\n\nFinal:", final)

        asyncio.run(main())
        ```
      </Tab>
    </Tabs>

    <Note>
      `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings` constructs a `TracerProvider`, registers the span processor pipeline, sets the global OTel tracer provider, and forwards itself to pydantic-ai's `Agent(instrument=...)`. The Confident AI API key is read automatically from the `CONFIDENT_API_KEY` environment variable or from `deepeval login` — you only need to pass `api_key=` explicitly if you manage keys programmatically.
    </Note>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Run Pydantic AI">
    Invoke your agent by executing the script:
    ```bash
    python main.py
    ```
    You can view the traces on [Confident AI](https://app.confident.ai) by clicking on the link printed in the console.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Advanced Usage

### Logging threads

Threads group related traces together and are useful for chat apps, agents, or any multi-turn interactions. You can learn more about [threads](/docs/llm-tracing/features/threads) here. Pass `thread_id` to `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings` to associate every trace from that agent with a thread.

<CodeBlocks>
  <CodeBlock title="Synchronous">
    ```python
    from pydantic_ai import Agent
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(
            thread_id="thread_id_1",
        ),
    )

    result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
    ```
  </CodeBlock>
  <CodeBlock title="Asynchronous">
    ```python
    import asyncio
    from pydantic_ai import Agent
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(
            thread_id="thread_id_1",
        ),
    )

    async def main():
        return await agent.run("What are LLMs?")

    result = asyncio.run(main())
    ```
  </CodeBlock>
  <CodeBlock title="Streaming">
    ```python
    import asyncio
    from pydantic_ai import Agent
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(
            thread_id="thread_id_1",
        ),
    )

    async def main():
        async with agent.run_stream("What are LLMs?") as result:
            async for chunk in result.stream_text(delta=True):
                print(chunk, end="", flush=True)
            final = await result.get_output()
            print("\n\nFinal:", final)

    asyncio.run(main())
    ```
  </CodeBlock>
</CodeBlocks>

### Trace attributes

You can attach trace-level attributes such as name, tags, metadata, and user ID to every trace produced by the agent. These are baked into `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings` as static defaults. They can be overridden at runtime using `update_current_trace(...)` from inside a tool body.

<CodeBlocks>
  <CodeBlock title="Synchronous">
    ```python
    from pydantic_ai import Agent
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(
            name="My Agent Trace",
            tags=["production", "v2"],
            metadata={"env": "production"},
            user_id="user_123",
            thread_id="thread_id_1",
        ),
    )

    result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
    ```
  </CodeBlock>
  <CodeBlock title="Asynchronous">
    ```python
    import asyncio
    from pydantic_ai import Agent
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(
            name="My Agent Trace",
            tags=["production", "v2"],
            metadata={"env": "production"},
            user_id="user_123",
            thread_id="thread_id_1",
        ),
    )

    async def main():
        return await agent.run("What are LLMs?")

    result = asyncio.run(main())
    ```
  </CodeBlock>
</CodeBlocks>

<Accordion title='View Trace Attributes'>
<ParamField path="api_key" type="str" required={false}>
  Your Confident AI API key. Falls back to the `CONFIDENT_API_KEY` environment variable or `deepeval login`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="name" type="str" required={false}>
  The default name for traces produced by this agent. [Learn more](/docs/llm-tracing/features/name).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="tags" type="List[str]" required={false}>
  String labels that help you group related traces. [Learn more](/docs/llm-tracing/features/tags).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="metadata" type="Dict" required={false}>
  Arbitrary metadata attached to each trace. At runtime, `update_current_trace(metadata=...)` is merged on top of this base. [Learn more](/docs/llm-tracing/features/metadata).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="thread_id" type="str" required={false}>
  Conversation or session ID for grouping multi-turn traces. [Learn more](/docs/llm-tracing/features/threads).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="user_id" type="str" required={false}>
  User identifier for user-level analytics. [Learn more](/docs/llm-tracing/features/users).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="metric_collection" type="str" required={false}>
  Name of the metric collection to run online evals against each trace.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="test_case_id" type="str" required={false}>
  Associates a trace with a specific test case.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="turn_id" type="str" required={false}>
  Identifies a specific turn within a multi-turn conversation.
</ParamField>

<Info>
  All attributes are optional. They work the same way as the [native tracing
  features](/docs/llm-tracing/introduction) on Confident AI. Any field set here
  is overridable at runtime via `update_current_trace(...)` from inside a tool body.
</Info>
</Accordion>

### Update trace attributes

You can enrich a trace mid-flight from inside a tool body using `update_current_trace`. This is useful when trace metadata depends on information only available during execution, such as a user ID resolved by a lookup tool.

```python main.py
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from deepeval.tracing import update_current_trace
from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

agent = Agent(
    "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
    instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
)

@agent.tool_plain
def lookup_user(user_id: str) -> str:
    # Enrich the trace with data resolved at runtime
    update_current_trace(
        user_id=user_id,
        metadata={"plan": "pro", "region": "us-east"},
    )
    return f"User {user_id} profile loaded."

result = agent.run_sync("Load my profile for user_42.")
```

<Note>
  `update_current_trace(...)` is safe to call from any tool body, including async tools and tools running in worker threads. The implicit trace context created by `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings` is propagated automatically via Python contextvars.
</Note>

### Update span attributes

You can attach span-level attributes such as metadata or a metric collection from inside a tool body using `update_current_span`. This is the primary way to configure per-tool evaluation behavior.

```python main.py
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from deepeval.tracing import update_current_span
from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

agent = Agent(
    "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
    instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
)

@agent.tool_plain
def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    update_current_span(
        metadata={"city": city, "source": "mock"},
        metric_collection="weather-tool-evals",
    )
    return f"{city}: sunny, 22°C"

result = agent.run_sync("What is the weather in Tokyo?")
```

### Logging prompts

If you are [managing prompts](/docs/llm-evaluation/prompt-management/version-prompts) on Confident AI and wish to log them, use `next_llm_span` to associate a `Prompt` with the next LLM span before calling your agent.

```python main.py
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from deepeval.prompt import Prompt
from deepeval.tracing import next_llm_span
from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

agent = Agent(
    "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
    instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
)

prompt = Prompt(alias="<prompt-alias>")
prompt.pull(version="00.00.01")

with next_llm_span(prompt=prompt):
    result = agent.run_sync(prompt.interpolate())
```

<Note>
  Be sure to **pull the prompt** before logging it, otherwise the prompt will
  not be visible on Confident AI. `next_llm_span` is one-shot — it is consumed
  by the next LLM span produced inside the `with` block.
</Note>

### Per-call trace context

To set per-call trace attributes (such as a different `user_id` per request), wrap each agent invocation in `with trace(...)`. This also switches routing to Confident AI's REST transport.

```python main.py
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from deepeval.tracing import trace
from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

agent = Agent(
    "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
    instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
)

with trace(user_id="user_42", thread_id="thread_1", name="my-trace"):
    result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
```

### Sending annotations

Send human annotations on traces or threads on Confident AI. Learn more about [sending annotations](/docs/human-in-the-loop/collect-feedback).

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Traces">
    ```python
    from deepeval.tracing import trace
    from deepeval.annotation import send_annotation
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
    )

    TRACE_UUID = None
    with trace() as current_trace:
        result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
        TRACE_UUID = current_trace.uuid

    send_annotation(
        trace_uuid=TRACE_UUID,
        rating=1,
    )
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Threads">
    ```python
    from deepeval.tracing import trace
    from deepeval.annotation import send_annotation
    from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

    agent = Agent(
        "openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
    )

    THREAD_ID = "thread_id_1"
    with trace(thread_id=THREAD_ID):
        result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")

    send_annotation(
        thread_id=THREAD_ID,
        rating=1,
    )
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Evals Usage

### Online evals

You can run [online evals](/docs/llm-tracing/online-evals) on your Pydantic AI agent. Online evals run evaluations on all incoming traces on Confident AI's servers and are the recommended approach for production agents.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Create metric collection">
    Create a metric collection on [Confident AI](https://app.confident.ai) with the metrics you want to use to evaluate your agent.
    <Frame caption="Create metric collection">
      <video
        autoPlay
        loop
        muted
        data-video="metrics.createCollection"
        type="video/mp4"
      />
    </Frame>
    <Warning>
      Your metric collection should only contain metrics that evaluate the input and output of the span or trace you are targeting.
    </Warning>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Run evals">
    You can run online evals at the trace level or the span level. Pass the `metric_collection` parameter to the appropriate target.

    <Tabs>
      <Tab title="Trace">
        Pass `metric_collection` to `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings` to evaluate every trace produced by the agent.

        ```python main.py
        from pydantic_ai import Agent
        from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

        agent = Agent(
            model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
            system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
            instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(
                metric_collection="my_trace_collection",
            ),
        )

        result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
        ```
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="Agent Span">
        Use `next_agent_span` from `deepeval.tracing` to attach a metric collection to the agent span before the run. This is the only way to configure agent-span-level evals, since user code does not execute inside agent spans.

        ```python main.py
        from pydantic_ai import Agent
        from deepeval.tracing import next_agent_span
        from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

        agent = Agent(
            model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
            system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
            instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
        )

        with next_agent_span(metric_collection="my_agent_collection"):
            result = agent.run_sync("What are LLMs?")
        ```

        <Note>
          `next_agent_span(...)` is one-shot — it is consumed by the next agent span only. To apply the same collection to every run, wrap each call individually or use the trace-level `metric_collection` on `DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings`.
        </Note>
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="Tool Span">
        Call `update_current_span` from inside the tool body to attach a metric collection to that tool's span.

        ```python main.py
        from pydantic_ai import Agent
        from deepeval.tracing import update_current_span
        from deepeval.integrations.pydantic_ai import DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings

        agent = Agent(
            model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
            system_prompt="Be concise, reply with one sentence.",
            instrument=DeepEvalInstrumentationSettings(),
        )

        @agent.tool_plain
        def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
            update_current_span(metric_collection="my_tool_collection")
            return f"{city}: sunny"

        result = agent.run_sync("What is the weather in Tokyo?")
        ```
      </Tab>
    </Tabs>

  </Step>
  <Success>
    All incoming traces and spans will now be evaluated using metrics from your metric collection.
  </Success>
</Steps>

You can view eval results on [Confident AI](https://app.confident.ai) by clicking on the link printed in the console.

<Frame>
  <video data-video="evaluation.singleTurnE2E" controls autoPlay playsInline />
</Frame>
